referenceProvenance: downstream Windows onscreen-client WinUI probe screenshots (private QA evidence)
evidenceBoundary: Real Windows reference PNGs, macOS runtime PNGs, and pixel-diff PNGs/JSON stay in the private QA evidence location and are never committed to the runtime repository. This manifest carries only sanitized scenario names and metrics.
maxChannelDeltaPolicy: maxChannelDelta stays diagnostic at 255; one high-contrast glyph edge can hit 255 even when the route is visually close.

scenarios.routeSelectionState: Signed-out login route; no navigation rail selection to reconcile.
mainVisualGaps: Login stack anchored top-left instead of centered narrow WinUI column.
Input fields and button too wide; PasswordBox mask density differs.
TextBox focus underline and clear affordance differ; progress bar width differs.

scenarios.routeSelectionState: Status route; rail selection state differs from the Windows reference (tracked as route/state fidelity).
mainVisualGaps: InfoBar severity surfaces, close button, and icon fill differ.
Title/message baselines differ.
Progress bar width and ring placement differ.
Conversation list and detail panes boxed and wider; selected row chrome differs.
Multiline TextBox height, inner padding, and bottom alignment differ; send button width differs.
InfoBar inside detail uses Mac strip chrome instead of filled severity surface.
NavigationView selected state, icon weight, and pane background differ.
List/detail carding, selected row fill, and detail border differ.
Progress bar width and page alignment differ.

scenarios.routeSelectionState: Admin route; rail selection state differs from the Windows reference (tracked as route/state fidelity).
mainVisualGaps: Native success InfoBar is a filled green band while Mac uses bordered strip chrome.
List/detail carding and selected row differ.
Action button density and widths differ.

scenarios.routeSelectionState: Admin workbench route; rail selection state differs from the Windows reference (tracked as route/state fidelity).
mainVisualGaps: Search field focus, clear/search glyphs, and command label/icon behavior differ.
List/detail cards and status InfoBar severity fill differ.
Command actions render icon-only instead of native label/chrome.

scenarios.routeSelectionState: Workbench command/search frame; rail selection state differs from the Windows reference (tracked as route/state fidelity).
mainVisualGaps: Search lacks the native focused underline and trailing clear/search glyph pattern.
Command actions render as icon-only instead of native label/chrome.
Same visual frame as workbench with simplified chrome.

scenarios.routeSelectionState: Settings route; rail selection state differs from the Windows reference (tracked as route/state fidelity).
mainVisualGaps: Settings form card radius and stroke differ.
Input widths, focus underline, clear icon, and field vertical rhythm differ.
Button natural width differs.

sharedGaps.gap: Mac content tends to start at the top-left or uses larger pane/card extents while Windows content is narrower, centered, or aligned to WinUI page columns.
Text is present and usually legible, but vertical rhythm, line baselines, weight, and compact caption/body spacing differ.
TextBox, PasswordBox, Button, CheckBox, InfoBar, ProgressBar, ProgressRing, NavigationViewItem, ListView, and AppBarButton use approximate chrome.
sharedGaps.evidence: All scenarios, strongest in login and status states.

sharedGaps.gap: Mac rows, cards, text inputs, and buttons often occupy more vertical or horizontal space than Windows.
Accent and severity colors are present, but selection fills, InfoBar severity backgrounds, disabled tracks, pane backgrounds, and subtle surfaces differ.
Mac adds rounded card borders around list/detail regions where Windows often uses flatter pane separation or a single detail border.
sharedGaps.evidence: Shell, messages, admin dashboard, workbench, settings.

sharedGaps.gap: Mac boxes the list/detail panes, uses stronger selected-row blue, and draws separators differently.
Focus underline, clear button, search glyph placement, password masking density, caret/selection, and natural field width differ.
Mac search lacks the native focused underline and trailing clear/search glyph pattern, and command actions render as icon-only instead of native label/chrome.
Mac InfoBars use vertical strips and bordered white surfaces; Windows fills severity surfaces and shows close affordance. Progress placement and sizing differ.
sharedGaps.evidence: Status states and admin status surfaces.

sharedGaps.gap: Some route screenshots show different rail selection state between Windows and Mac, especially Admin, Workbench, Status, Profile, and Settings routes.
sharedGaps.evidence: Admin dashboard, workbench, command-search, status, settings.

thresholdLadder.purpose: Baseline: current failing evidence is complete and reviewable.
Coarse route alignment: remove top-left/full-width/page-frame drift.
thresholdLadder.manualGate: Content appears in the same route regions; no missing major surface.

thresholdLadder.purpose: Layout and density parity: align page columns, list/detail widths, row heights, and form natural sizing.
Control-family parity: match core chrome for forms, buttons, list rows, status, progress, and navigation.
thresholdLadder.manualGate: Native-visible chrome differences are minor, not structural.

thresholdLadder.purpose: Conservative native-comparison pass: pass the existing downstream comparison contract.
Premium production promotion: promote only after public component crops and private route screenshots agree.
thresholdLadder.changedPixels: <= 35% for app routes; <= 24% for focused command/status/form routes when achievable
thresholdLadder.manualGate: Manual review signs off no route/content mismatch, no missing affordance, no misleading status, no text overlap.

manualInspectionCriteria: Wrong route content or wrong selected navigation state versus native reference.
Missing user-visible control, status, command, close affordance, glyph, or label.
Text overlap, clipped critical text, leaked password content, or unreadable line height.
Control chrome communicates a different state than Windows, especially selected, focused, disabled, error, warning, success, loading, or checked.
Screenshot was generated without real Windows PNG provenance or with non-PNG replacement evidence.
Improvement comes only from loosening thresholds or hiding unsupported diagnostics.
